page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 6455B processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a high base clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory and compute capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-core metrics, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2232.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.83 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 224.53 GB/sec for reads and 104.52 GB/sec for writes. The processor's advanced instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, contribute to strong performance in LLM inference, delivering 381.58 tokens/sec for text generation on small models and 28.25 tokens/sec on medium models.

Given its high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and dense DDR5 memory configuration, the r7iz.8xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile that balances high-compute speeds with memory capacity. This makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring fast single-thread execution alongside large memory footprints. It is well-suited for relational databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, real-time data analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but provides a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2232.0 and strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 224.53 GB/sec. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, this server delivers strong performance in text processing, image processing, and LLM inference. It is optimized for memory-intensive workloads requiring high CPU clock speeds, such as relational databases, enterprise applications, and real-time data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor, and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and analytics.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, and 256 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized platform featuring DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel instruction sets for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processor
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance and strong uncached memory bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated execution
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Designed for relational databases, enterprise applications, and data analytics
